description: Assign, load, or get information on an mpoint constitutive model. This command associates a constitutive model with specified mpoint. It also allows querying of properties and states of constitutive models, and dynamic loading of user-defined mechanical models as DLLs.

keywords.name: hydration-drucker-prager
keywords.description: Assign a mechanical material model to all mpoints in the range. The mechanical constitutive models are described in Constitutive Models , and the creep models are in Creep Constitutive Models . Null Mechanical Models Assign the null model. For most purposes, null models are ignored by default (see the Null model description). Elastic Mechanical Models Assign the transversely-anisotropic elastic model (see the Anisotropic model description). Assign the isotropic elastic model (see the Elastic Model description). Assign the orthotropic elastic model (see the Orthotropic model description). Elastic-Plastic Mechanical Models Assign the columnar-basalt elasto-plasticity model, known also as COMBA model (see the Columnar-Basalt model description). Assign the concrete elasto-plasticity model (see the Concrete model description). Assign the cap-yield elasto-plasticity model, known also as CYSoil model (see the CYSoil model description). Assign the simplified cap yield elasto-plasticity model, known also as CHSoil model (see the CHSoil model description). Assign the double-yield (cap) elasto-plasticity model (see the Double-Yield model description). Assign the Drucker-Prager elasto-plasticity model (see the Drucker-Prager model description). Assign the Hoek-Brown elasto-plasticity model (see the Hoek-Brown model description). Assign the Hoek-Brown-PAC elasto-plasticity model (see the Hoek-Brow-PAC model description). Assign the modified Cam-Clay elasto-plasticity model (see the modified Cam-Clay model description). Assign the Mohr-Coulomb elasto-plasticity model (see the Mohr-Coulomb model description). Assign the Mohr-Coulomb elasto-plasticity model with tensile strain crack tracking, known also as the MohrT model (see the Mohr-Coulomb-Tension model description). Assign the norsand model — a critical state model applicable to soils in which particle to particle interactions are controlled by contact forces and slips rather than bonds (see the NorSand model description). Assign the elasto-plasticity model with shear and volumetric hardening, known also as PH model (see the Plastic-Hardening model description). Assign the soft-soil model for soil with significant compression (see the Soft-Soil model description). Assign the strain-softening/hardening elasto-plasticity model with bilinear failure and ubiquitous joints, known also as the SUBI model (see the Softening-Ubiquitous model description). Assign the strain-softening/hardening elasto-plasticity model (see the Strain-Softening model description). Assign the Mohr-Coulomb elasto-plasticity model considering wetting-induced deformation (see the Swell model description). Assign the Mohr-Coulomb elasto-plasticity model with a plane of weakness, known also as UBI model (see the Ubiquitous-Joint model description). Assign the model with a plane of weakness and with anisotropic elasticity (see the Ubiquitous-Anisotropic model description). Assign the Von-Mises elasto-plasticity model commonly for metal-like materials(see the Von-Mises model description). Dynamic Option Model Assign the dynamic pore-pressure generation elasto-plasticity model (only available for the dynamic option; see the Finn model description). Assign the p2psand model refers to a Practical TWO-surface Plastic SAND constitutive model for general 3D geotechnical earthquake engineering application (see the P2PSand model description). IMASS Option Model Assign the IMASS model (only available for the imass option; see the IMASS model description). Creep Option Model Assign the classical viscoelastic model known as the Burgers substance which composed of a Kelvin model and a Maxwell model (only available for the creep option; see the Burgers model description). Assign the viscoplastic model combining the Burgers model and the Mohr-Coulomb model (only available for the creep option; see the Burgers-Mohr model description). Assign the columnar-basalt elasto-plasticity model, known also as COMBA model (see the Columnar-Basalt model description). Assign the classical viscoelastic model known as the Maxwell substance, known also as Maxwell model (only available for the creep option; see the Maxwell model description). Assign the two-component power law model used for mining applications (only available for the creep option; see the Power model description). Assign the viscoplastic model combining the two-component Power model and the Mohr-Coulomb model (only available for the creep option; see the Power-Mohr model description). Assign the viscoplastic model combining the two-component Power model and the Ubiquitous-Joint model (only available for the creep option; see the Power-Ubiqitous mdoel description). Assign the soft-soil-creep model for soil with time-dependent behavior such as the creep that develops during the secondary compression (see the Soft-Soil-Creep model description). Assign a reference creep model commonly used in thermomechanical analyses associated with studies for the underground isolation of nuclear waste in salt (only available for the creep option; see the WIPP model description). Assign the viscoplastic model combining the WIPP model and the Drucker-Prager model (only available for the creep option, see the WIPP-Drucker model description). Assign the viscoplastic model modified from the WIPP model, and includes volumetric and deviatoric compaction behavior for salt-like materials (only available for the creep option; see the WIPP-Salt model description). Thermal Option Model Assign the modified Drucker-Prager for hydration (only available for the thermal option; see see the Hydration-Drucker-Prager model description).
